annotations.label: Fair coin flip information

anchor.snippet: if one flips a fair coin and does not yet know the outcome

annotations.note: The fair-coin entropy is the special case `binEntropy 2⁻¹ = log 2` (one bit in nats), but the general information/uncertainty concept of the example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nyquist's line-speed relation

anchor.snippet: W is the speed of transmission of intelligence

annotations.note: Nyquist's W = K log m line-speed relation has no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hartley's measure of information

anchor.snippet: where S was the number of possible symbols, and n the number of symbols in a transmission

annotations.note: Hartley information H = n log S is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p log p convention at zero

anchor.snippet: an expression of the form p log p is considered by convention to be equal to zero

annotations.note: `negMulLog_zero : negMulLog 0 = 0` encodes exactly the convention that p·log p vanishes at p = 0 (via Mathlib's `Real.log 0 = 0`).

annotations.label: Shannon entropy

anchor.snippet: is defined as the expected value of the information content of the symbols

annotations.note: The summand `negMulLog` and special cases `binEntropy`/`qaryEntropy` exist, but the general Shannon entropy −∑ pᵢ log pᵢ of an arbitrary distribution is not defined.

annotations.label: Self-information (surprisal)

anchor.snippet: is known as its self-information or surprisal

annotations.note: There is no named self-information/surprisal `−log p(x)` definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Less probable symbol has larger surprisal

anchor.snippet: A less probable symbol has a larger surprisal

annotations.note: Strict monotonicity of `Real.log` gives that smaller probability yields larger −log p, but surprisal itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: 1000-bit transmission

anchor.snippet: if one transmits 1000 bits

annotations.note: This is an informal illustrative example with no Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Maximum entropy at equiprobability

anchor.snippet: it is maximized when all the messages in the message space are equiprobable

annotations.note: Only the binary case (`binEntropy ≤ log 2`, maximal at the equiprobable p = ½) is formalized, not the general uniform-maximizes-entropy theorem.

annotations.label: Total entropy of i.i.d. source

anchor.snippet: the total entropy of the message is

annotations.note: Additivity of entropy over an i.i.d. source is not formalized since general entropy is absent.

annotations.label: Binary entropy function

anchor.snippet: is the binary entropy function

annotations.note: `Real.binEntropy p = p·log p⁻¹ + (1−p)·log (1−p)⁻¹` is exactly the binary entropy function.

annotations.label: Binary entropy maximum at p=0.5

anchor.snippet: reaching its maximum of 1 bit when p = 0.5

annotations.note: `binEntropy_le_log_two` plus `binEntropy_eq_log_two ↔ p = 2⁻¹` give the maximum log 2 (= 1 bit) attained uniquely at p = ½.

annotations.label: Joint entropy

anchor.snippet: is merely the entropy of their pairing

annotations.note: Joint entropy H(X,Y) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Joint entropy of independent variables

anchor.snippet: then their joint entropy is the sum of their individual entropies

annotations.note: Additivity of joint entropy under independence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chess piece position

anchor.snippet: represents the position of a chess piece

annotations.note: This is an informal illustrative example with no Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Joint entropy as subcase of entropy

anchor.snippet: joint entropy is just a subcase of entropy where the random variable is a vector

annotations.note: Neither entropy nor joint entropy of random variables is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional entropy

anchor.snippet: is the average conditional entropy over

annotations.note: Conditional entropy H(X|Y) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chain rule property of conditional entropy

anchor.snippet: A basic property of this form of conditional entropy is that

annotations.note: The entropy chain rule H(X|Y) = H(X,Y) − H(Y) is not formalized; the existing `KullbackLeibler.ChainRule` concerns KL divergence, not entropy.

annotations.label: Mutual information

anchor.snippet: measures the amount of information that can be obtained about one random variable by observing another

annotations.note: Mutual information I(X;Y) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basic property of mutual information

anchor.snippet: A basic property of the mutual information is that

annotations.note: Identities like I(X;Y) = H(X) − H(X|Y) are not formalized since mutual information and entropy are absent.

annotations.label: Mutual information is symmetric

anchor.snippet: Mutual information is symmetric

annotations.note: Symmetry of mutual information is not formalized since mutual information itself is undefined.

annotations.label: Mutual information as KL divergence

anchor.snippet: Mutual information can be expressed as the average Kullback–Leibler divergence

annotations.note: `klDiv` (the RHS building block) exists, but mutual information and its identity as a KL divergence between joint and product measures are not formalized.

annotations.label: Kullback–Leibler divergence

anchor.snippet: is a way of comparing two distributions

annotations.note: `InformationTheory.klDiv μ ν` defines KL divergence for general finite measures, generalizing the discrete ∑ p log(p/q).

annotations.label: KL divergence is not a metric

anchor.snippet: KL divergence is not a true metric since it is not symmetric

annotations.note: Mathlib has `klDiv` and `klDiv_eq_zero_iff`, but the negative claim that it is asymmetric / not a metric is not stated.

annotations.label: Directed information

anchor.snippet: is an information theory measure that quantifies the information flow

annotations.note: Directed information is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Directed information is not symmetric

anchor.snippet: directed information is not symmetric

annotations.note: Asymmetry of directed information is not formalized since the measure itself is undefined.

annotations.label: Information rate

anchor.snippet: Information rate is the average entropy per symbol

annotations.note: Information (entropy) rate is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Channel capacity

anchor.snippet: this maximum mutual information is called the channel capacity

annotations.note: Channel capacity (sup of mutual information) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noisy-channel coding theorem

anchor.snippet: there exists a code of length N and rate ≥ R and a decoding algorithm

annotations.note: Shannon's noisy-channel coding theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Capacity of the binary symmetric channel

anchor.snippet: The BSC has a capacity of

annotations.note: The binary entropy term in C = 1 − H_b(p) exists as `binEntropy`, but channel capacity is not defined so the BSC capacity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Capacity of the binary erasure channel

anchor.snippet: The capacity of the BEC is

annotations.note: The BEC capacity C = 1 − p is not formalized since channel capacity is undefined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Capacity of channels with memory

anchor.section: Channels with memory and directed information
anchor.snippet: the capacity is given by the mutual information rate when there is no feedback available

annotations.note: Capacity of channels with memory via mutual information rate is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fungible information

anchor.snippet: Fungible information is the information for which the means of encoding is not important

annotations.note: Fungible information has no definition in Mathlib.
